title: No field-granular schema-edit op; changing one schema property requires whole-document write-schema replace
description: Schema evolution exposes only whole-document write-schema (operation create|replace); there is no field-granular schema edit. Changing one property (add/rename/retype, bump version) requires supplying and overwriting the entire schema JSON, unlike block items which have update-block-item for field-level updates.

evidence.reference: write-schema operation enum is create|replace, whole-document; no granular per-property operation
update-block-item provides match+updates field-granular item edits — the absent analogue for schema documents

impact: A one-field schema change carries whole-document blast radius: an unrelated edit in the replaced JSON lands silently, and the error surface grows as the PM-model arc evolves many schemas through repeated full-document replaces.
proposed_resolution: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
